title: Our watchlist (anything goes) — shared partner-watchlist, Dec 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • A shared watchlist modified Dec 14 2025 → Mar 7 2026 — almost certainly Yahir + Emily's joint queue. Annotations like "(never seen it) (same!!!)" show two people typing into the same note.
  • Mix:
    • Romantic / drama: When Harry Met Sally, The Summer I Turned Pretty, Forrest Gump, La La Land, Blue Valentine, People We Meet on Vacation, 13 Going on 30, Dirty Dancing.
    • Iconic-thriller: Joker, Birdman, American History X, Sopranos, Matrix (twice), Casablanca, No Country for Old Men, NO.
    • Animation-comfort: Madagascar, Sing, Kung Fu Panda 1+2, Lego Movie.
    • Kids/nostalgic: ET, Ready Player One, The Wild Robot, The Dinosaurs on Netflix.
    • High-pop: Diddy Documentary, Marty Supreme, Landman, Fifty Shades of Gray.
    • Spanish-language: Jamón Jamón, Tardes de Soledad.
    • Exotic: "Irl musical/play/live theatre."

Key marker

"(never seen it) (same!!!)" — confirms multi-typist authoring.

Why it matters in the graph

  • Confirms the relationship dynamic with Emily is collaborative enough to share a notes-app document for months. Pairs with the 2025 09 15 gifts for people gift-attention and runs opposite to the misalignment audit in 2026 03 24 alignment misalignment — useful for tracking the arc-shape.
  • Diff vs Yahir's solo 2023 watchlist (2023 07 15 things to watch):
    • Solo: Magnolia, Tree of Life, Vivarium, Gummo (auteur + weird).
    • Shared: When Harry Met Sally, La La Land, Blue Valentine, Sopranos (rom-com + prestige).
    • The shared list is more genre-romantic and less weird — the partner-effect on cinematic taste.
  • Spanish-language picks (Jamón Jamón, Tardes de Soledad) align with Yahir's Spanish-practice in 2026 01 29 billy vocab list.
  • Diddy Documentary on the list is 2025-cultural-moment timestamp — tracking the actual zeitgeist they're in.

Open questions

  • How many of these have actually been watched? Worth a follow-up.
  • Whose pick is "The Dinosaurs on Netflix" — Emily-coded or Yahir-coded?
